title: Boulder Co Vlog 3/29/18 Roller Rode 2 hrs, 31.38m Super Tired.

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about a difficult two-hour roller ride covering roughly 31 miles, struggling with low appetite, fatigue, and stomach issues he attributes to a long period of smoking marijuana and not exercising. He talks about his recovery eating (pizza, juice, protein drink), his current low power output, and plans to continue riding the next day.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: prolonged marijuana use hurt his appetite and ability to exercise, and he's now paying for it physically
attributes current struggle partly to aging but mostly to deconditioning from inactivity
plans to keep riding and build up to four hours a day despite the difficulty

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I forgot to do the part with sewing my chest, but I'll do it tomorrow.

claims.claim: I'm considering how I feel about showing my chest for a day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I like experimenting a little bit.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got up kind of late today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wrote for two hours today.

claims.claim: I developed a really low appetite from smoking marijuana and not exercising.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Marijuana made me unable to perpetually live, just not being able to swallow food.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had a fruit protein drink and tomato soup for breakfast.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wrote for two hours after breakfast.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode about 30 to 35 miles on my flutchiner.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can ride at about 17 miles an hour, 150 watts, but by the end of two hours, I was down to 100 watts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I fell asleep after riding.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got up and had Mutsus.

claims.claim: Freshly made pizza is the best thing for me to eat right now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I spent 18 bucks on pizza and managed to eat almost half of it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't know if I'm going to ride later today because my stomach feels bad.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The main problem is my stomach, not my legs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can't eat and I don't have any energy, which is fucked up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Two years ago, I would have been able to jump right into this kind of activity.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Three years ago, I did three days in a row of riding miles a day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I hit a wall where you go downhill fast, and it seems like an age thing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's not really an age thing, but more like aging competition.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm not excited, but I also feel retarded.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to organize files on my computer because I watch lots of files while I ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to organize and make something validated out of no-thought riding.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I ride four hours a day and keep it up, I'll eventually climb up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't want the climbing up to be a two-paying full.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm doing the video now so I don't pass up and not do it.
